step_types.name: agent
step_types.semantics: Dispatches a pi subprocess with a compiled prompt (agent spec + templates + resolved input). The subprocess runs autonomously, produces output, and exits. Output is captured and validated against schema if declared.

fields.name: agent
fields.description: Name of the agent spec to invoke

fields.name: model
fields.description: Model override for this step (overrides agent spec model)

step_types.output_behavior: Output extracted from last assistant message. Structured JSON read from output file if schema-bound. Text output from message content. Written to outputs/<stepName>.json or author-declared output.path.

step_types.name: gate
step_types.semantics: Async gate execution with process groups. Always returns completed with { passed: true/false }. Both outcomes are valid — gate judges a condition. Supports onPass/onFail handlers.

fields.name: gate
fields.description: Gate configuration with agent, onPass, onFail

step_types.output_behavior: Always returns { passed: boolean }. Gate steps never fail — both pass and fail are valid outcomes.

step_types.name: transform
step_types.semantics: Pure expression mapping — no LLM invocation. Resolves mapping fields via ${{ }} expressions. Used for data reshaping between steps.

fields.name: transform
fields.description: Mapping of output field names to ${{ }} expressions

step_types.output_behavior: Produces a JSON object with fields resolved from expressions. No subprocess spawned.

step_types.name: loop
step_types.semantics: Loop iteration with accumulated evidence. Receives dispatchAgent callback. Each iteration can observe previous iteration results. Supports maxIterations and onExhausted handling.

fields.name: loop
fields.description: Loop configuration with agent, maxIterations, until, onExhausted

step_types.output_behavior: Output from the final successful iteration. Each iteration accumulates evidence for the next.

step_types.name: parallel
step_types.semantics: Executes multiple steps concurrently. executeParallelLayer() processes a set of independent steps. Receives executeSingleStep callback for each parallel step.

fields.name: parallel
fields.description: Parallel step configuration with steps array

step_types.output_behavior: Each parallel step produces its own output. Results collected as a map of step name to result.

step_types.name: pause
step_types.semantics: Returns completed immediately. The executor then sets the workflow status to paused. Workflow-authored deliberate pause points for human review or intervention.
step_types.output_behavior: No output. Step completes, workflow pauses. Resume via Ctrl+J or /workflow resume.

step_types.name: forEach
step_types.semantics: Iterates over an array. Resolves the array from an expression, binds each element via the as: field, adds forEach.index and forEach.length to the expression scope. Collects outputs as an array.

fields.name: forEach
fields.description: forEach configuration: items (array expression), as (binding name), step (agent/transform to run per item)

fields.name: when
fields.description: Global conditional — applies before iteration starts, not per-iteration

step_types.output_behavior: Collects outputs from all iterations as an array. Each iteration's output is appended.

step_types.name: command
step_types.semantics: Runs sh -c <command>. Captures stdout as JSON or text. Fails on non-zero exit code (unlike gate). Supports ${{ }} expressions in command string, timeout, signal cancellation.
step_types.output_behavior: Captures stdout. If stdout is valid JSON, parsed as structured output. Otherwise wrapped as { text: '...' }. Non-zero exit code marks step as failed.

capabilities.name: Whole object reference
capabilities.description: Passes the entire object, not just a field

scope_keys.description: Completed step results (output, textOutput, status, usage, durationMs)

limitations: No arithmetic or string concatenation — use transform steps for computation
No function calls — planned as a future gap (expression-functions)
Adjacent ${{ }}${{ }} in a single string can misparse (whole-value regex limitation)

subprocess_environment.description: Same cwd as the main pi instance (the project directory)
Full process.env from the main process, including API keys, PATH, etc.
Same .pi/ project config and ~/.pi/agent/ user config (pi discovers these from cwd and home directory)
By default, normal discovery. If agent spec declares extensions: [...], only those are loaded (--no-extensions + explicit --extension flags)
If agent spec declares skills: [...], only those are loaded (--no-skills + explicit injection). Otherwise, normal skill discovery.
Subprocess writes session file to --session-dir <run-dir>/sessions/. Captures full session log for tracing.
Ignored (stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe']). Subprocess cannot prompt user — intentional for autonomous workflow steps.

event_stream.description: Message with content and usage data
Agent run complete — contains all messages

dispatch_protocol.completion_detection: Process exit is the definitive completion signal (exit code 0 = success, non-zero = failure). The agent_end event fires before exit and contains all messages, but process exit also catches unexpected termination.
dispatch_protocol.output_extraction: Walk the messages array backward from the agent_end event, find the last assistant message, extract its text content. If step has output schema, agent writes JSON to a file — executor reads that file after process exits and validates against schema.
dispatch_protocol.usage_tracking: Each message_end event for assistant messages includes a usage field with token counts and cost. The executor accumulates these across turns.
dispatch_protocol.error_detection: If exit code is non-zero, or if the last tool result contains error patterns (permission denied, segmentation fault, etc.), the step is marked failed.
dispatch_protocol.model_override: Step-level model overrides agent-level model. Subprocess receives via --models <model-id>. If neither is set, pi uses its default.
dispatch_protocol.tool_filtering: Agent specs declare tools: [read, bash, grep]. Dispatch separates built-in tool names (go to --tools) from extension paths containing / or ending in .ts/.js (go to --extension).

contents.name: state.json
contents.description: Current execution state: which steps completed, their outputs, workflow status

contents.name: sessions/
contents.description: Full session logs from each step subprocess (one .session.json per step)

contents.name: outputs/
contents.description: Step outputs as schema-validated JSON (one file per step, default: outputs/<stepName>.json)

contents.name: metrics.json
contents.description: Aggregated cost/token tracking across all steps

state_management.persistence_method: State persisted after each step via atomic write (tmp file + rename). State write failures are fatal — state.json is the sole checkpoint for resume. Output write failures are non-fatal — step data is in memory for downstream expressions.

widget.description: Persistent compact view showing current step, progress, cost. Visible while the workflow runs.

dashboard.description: Detailed view toggled by shortcut. Shows all steps with status, outputs, timing, cost breakdown. Scrollable.

keybindings.action: Pause workflow after current step completes

forms.name: template
forms.description: Inline interpolation — a single string with ${{ }} expressions that resolve against CompletionScope. For simple cases where the result message is a formatted string.

forms.name: message + include
forms.description: Instruction text with data attachments. message is the text shown to the LLM. include lists scope paths whose values are attached as structured data. For structured data injection into LLM context.

scope.description: Aggregated usage (input tokens, output tokens, cost)

events.name: workflow:step_end
events.payload: step name, status, output, usage, duration

artifacts.description: Persistent files written to user-defined paths after workflow completion. Each workflow owns a directory under .pi/workflow-runs/<workflow-name>/. Run state goes in runs/ subdirectories; artifacts live at the top level as the workflow's persistent, findable output.

spec_fields.name: path
spec_fields.description: Destination path relative to the workflow's output directory. May contain ${{ }} expressions (e.g. ${{ runId }} for history accumulation, or a fixed name for the latest pattern).

spec_fields.name: from
spec_fields.description: Expression path resolving to the data to write (structured output, text output, or any scope value). Resolves against CompletionScope.

spec_fields.name: schema
spec_fields.description: Optional JSON Schema for validation before writing. Reuses existing schema validation infrastructure.

patterns.name: latest
patterns.description: Fixed filename (e.g. latest.md) overwritten each run. Always current without knowing run ID.

patterns.name: history
patterns.description: Filename with ${{ runId }} accumulates history. Past runs preserved for comparison.

naming.concept: One run of an agent (prompt in → response out, possibly multi-turn)
naming.name: agent run

naming.concept: A session that was interrupted before completion
naming.name: interrupted session

naming.concept: Workflow execution without user intervention between steps
naming.name: autonomous execution

naming.concept: The event fired when an agent run completes
naming.name: agent_end
naming.rationale: pi's existing event name, accurate as-is

key_fields.name: triggerTurn
key_fields.description: Inject result into conversation and trigger LLM response (default: true)

core_types.name: StepSpec
core_types.description: Specification for a single workflow step

core_types.name: StepResult
core_types.description: Result from executing a single step

core_types.name: WorkflowResult
core_types.description: Result from executing a complete workflow
